steps.title: Set up the unit conversions needed
steps.content:
We're converting from square feet per hour to square meters per minute. This requires two separate conversions:
  • Area units: square feet to square meters
  • Time rate: per hour to per minute
Since area is 2-dimensional, the linear scale factor (1 m = 3.28 ft) must be squared:
$$1 \text{ m}^2 = (3.28)^2 \text{ ft}^2 = 10.7584 \text{ ft}^2$$

steps.title: Apply both conversions to the rate
steps.content:
Multiply by the area conversion and divide by the time conversion:
$$270 \text{ ft}^2/\text{hour} \times \frac{1 \text{ m}^2}{10.7584 \text{ ft}^2} \times \frac{1 \text{ hour}}{60 \text{ min}} = \frac{270}{10.7584 \times 60} = \frac{270}{645.504} = 0.4182 \text{ m}^2/\text{min}$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choice
steps.content: The calculated rate of 0.4182 square meters per minute is closest to 0.42. That matches choice A — A is correct.
